new_regime.source_section: Income-tax Act 2025 sections 202(1) [slabs], 156(2) [rebate], 19(1) Table Sl.No.2(a) [standard deduction]

old_regime.source_section: Income-tax Act 2025 sections 156(1) [rebate], 19(1) Table Sl.No.2(b) [standard deduction], 123 [80C-equivalent], 126(2) [80D-equivalent]
old_regime.note: Basic exemption and slab rates (2.5L/5L/10L breakpoints, 5%/20%/30% rates, and the raised 3L exemption for resident senior citizens aged 60+) are not specified in the Act text itself -- it defers rate figures to the annual Finance Act. These values are carried over from established convention and should be reconfirmed against the Finance Act for the relevant year before relying on them for an actual filing. Super-senior citizens (80+) get a further-raised exemption in real practice but that age tier is out of scope for v1 -- TaxInput only has a single is_senior_citizen flag.

capital_gains.source_section: Income-tax Act 2025 sections 196(1) [equity STCG], 198(2) [equity LTCG + 1.25L exemption], 197(1) [general LTCG], 197(3) [land/building grandfathering], 194 Table Sl.No.4 [virtual digital assets]

surcharge.note: Surcharge rate is not in the Act text -- s.7974 only says tax 'shall be increased by a surcharge, if any, levied by any Central Act', deferring the rate to the annual Finance Act, same pattern as old-regime slabs and cess_rate above. Brackets and the 15% cap on equity STT STCG/LTCG and general/land-building LTCG (s.196/198/197) -- and the new regime's 25% ceiling with no 37% slab -- are carried over from established Finance Act convention (FY2023-24 onward) and must be reconfirmed against the actual Finance Act for TY2026-27. VDA/crypto gains (s.194 Table Sl.No.4) are NOT covered by the 15% cap and are surcharged at the ordinary bracket rate.

house_property.source_section: Income-tax Act 2025 ss.20-25 [house property income], 22(1)/(2) [deductions & self-occupied interest cap], 109(1)(b) [Rs.2L set-off cap, old regime], 110 [carry-forward], 202(2)(a)(v) [new regime disallows self-occupied interest deduction], 202(2)(b)(ii) [new regime blocks HP loss set-off against other heads]

nps.source_section: Income-tax Act 2025 s.124(1)-(4) [employer & self NPS contribution deductions], 202(2)(a)(xii) [new regime exception preserving s.124(1)/(2)]

cess_note: Health & Education Cess rate is set by the annual Finance Act and is not present in the Act text; 4% is carried over from established convention. Cess is levied on income-tax plus surcharge.
